steps.title: Find the scale factor
steps.content:
The volumes are $392\pi$ for cylinder A and $10{,}584\pi$ for cylinder B. Their volume ratio is
$$\frac{10{,}584\pi}{392\pi}=27$$
For similar solids, volume scales by the cube of the linear scale factor, so the linear scale factor is $3$.

steps.title: Find cylinder A height
steps.content:
Use the volume formula with radius $7$.
$$\pi(7)^2h=392\pi$$
$$49h=392$$
$$h=8$$

steps.title: Compute the surface areas
steps.content:
Cylinder A has surface area
$$2\pi(7)^2+2\pi(7)(8)=98\pi+112\pi=210\pi$$
So $k=210$. Surface area scales by the square of the linear scale factor, so cylinder B has surface area
$$9(210\pi)=1{,}890\pi$$
Thus $n=1{,}890$.
